title: Phase 1.5: Make VPS install + update a one-liner
summary: Wrap the existing docker-compose deploy in a curl-pipe-bash bootstrap script and a tiny update script so that standing up brainbot on a fresh VPS or applying a new commit is a single copy-pasted command.
outOfScope: GitHub Actions push-to-deploy (defer until manual deploys feel slow)
Building + pushing images to GHCR (Phase 2+ if registry-based deploy becomes worthwhile)
technicalConsiderations: Install script must be safe under curl-pipe-bash: no interactive prompts that break under a pipe (accept env vars as primary input, prompt only if stdin is a tty), set -euo pipefail
Never overwrite an existing /opt/brainbot/compose/.env (secrets live there)
Default install path /opt/brainbot is overridable via BRAINBOT_DIR env
Deploy strategy choice: git pull --ff-only vs git fetch + reset --hard origin/main — pick one (reset is more predictable for a deploy host that should never have local edits) and document
Auto-update via systemd timer is opt-in, not default
